title: AccessLecture Compliance Scoring Rubric
description: Scoring rubric grounded in WCAG 2.1 AA, Section 508, FCC caption quality standards, and DCMP Captioning Key. Each dimension maps to a specific regulatory requirement.

section_508.note: References WCAG 2.0 AA as technical standard; 29 U.S.C. 794d

ada_title_ii.note: Applies to public universities; WCAG 2.1 AA compliance required by 2026

uc_policy_is_12.note: UC system-wide Electronic Accessibility policy; references WCAG 2.x AA

dimensions.name: Caption Accuracy
dimensions.description: Captions must accurately represent spoken dialogue with no more than 1 error per 100 words (99% accuracy minimum).

measurements.name: Word Error Rate (WER)
measurements.description: Ratio of incorrect words to total words. Target: 99% or higher accuracy.

measurements.name: Proper Noun Accuracy
measurements.description: Names, places, and technical terms must be spelled correctly.

measurements.name: Grammar & Punctuation
measurements.description: Proper spelling, grammar, and punctuation throughout.

dimensions.failure_criteria: WCAG F8: Captions omitting dialogue or important sound effects.

dimensions.name: Caption Synchronization
dimensions.description: Captions must appear synchronized with their corresponding audio, within a tight tolerance.

measurements.name: Sync Offset
measurements.description: Maximum time difference between caption display and corresponding audio. FCC/Section 508 target: within 0.5 seconds.

measurements.name: Minimum Display Duration
measurements.description: Minimum 1 second 10 frames (~1.33s) per DCMP Captioning Key.

measurements.name: Maximum Display Duration
measurements.description: Maximum 6 seconds per caption block per DCMP Captioning Key.

measurements.name: Reading Speed
measurements.description: Upper-level educational content should not exceed 160 words per minute. DCMP specifies 120-160 wpm depending on audience level.

dimensions.name: Caption Completeness
dimensions.description: Captions must cover the entire duration of meaningful audio from beginning to end.

measurements.name: Audio Coverage
measurements.description: Percentage of spoken audio that has corresponding captions. All dialogue must be captioned.

measurements.name: Caption Gaps
measurements.description: No gaps longer than 5 seconds during active speech. Silent periods are acceptable.

measurements.name: Non-Speech Sound Descriptions
measurements.description: Music, laughter, applause, environmental sounds, and other meaningful non-speech audio must be captioned using bracket notation: [music], [laughter], [applause].

dimensions.failure_criteria: WCAG F8: Omitting dialogue or important sound effects is a failure.

dimensions.name: Speaker Identification
dimensions.description: When multiple speakers are present, each speaker must be clearly identified.

measurements.name: Speaker Labels Present
measurements.description: Every change in speaker must be indicated. Format: '>> Speaker Name:' or '[Speaker Name]:'

measurements.name: Speaker Label Consistency
measurements.description: Same speaker must always be identified the same way. No mixing of 'Prof. Smith' and 'Professor Smith'.

dimensions.name: Caption Formatting
dimensions.description: Captions must follow standard formatting rules for readability.
dimensions.standard: DCMP Captioning Key / Section 508 / FCC Placement

measurements.name: Line Length
measurements.description: Maximum 42 characters per line (DCMP recommends 32 for optimal readability). Hard maximum of 45.

measurements.name: Logical Line Breaks
measurements.description: Line breaks should occur at logical speech pauses. Do not break: modifiers from words, prepositional phrases, proper nouns, conjunctions from clauses, auxiliary verbs from main verbs.

measurements.name: Mixed Case
measurements.description: Use mixed case characters. ALL CAPS only for emphasis/shouting per DCMP.

dimensions.name: Visual Reference Accessibility
dimensions.description: Captions must flag or describe visual content that is referenced in speech but not visible to those relying solely on captions/audio.

measurements.name: Visual Reference Detection
measurements.description: Phrases like 'as you can see', 'look at this graph', 'this slide shows', 'over here' must be flagged as they are inaccessible without visual context.

verbatim.description: Preserves all speech exactly as spoken, including filler words and false starts. Only fixes clear transcription errors. Required by some strict interpretations of Section 508.

clean.description: Removes filler words (um, uh, like, you know), fixes grammar, and improves readability while preserving meaning. Preferred for educational accessibility and readability.
